pages.title: Thank You for Participating!
pages.description:
Congratulations on completing this chapter. We’d love to hear your reflections on your experience so far—what stood out, what supported your learning, and how we can continue to improve.

This short reflection will take less than 5 minutes and helps us ensure our sessions are impactful, relevant, and aligned with your goals.

Your honest feedback is essential. This is an opportunity to model authentic communication —your insights will help us shape and strengthen this learning journey together.

headings.heading: Based on your experience during this chapter, please rate the following statements.
This chapter is relevant to my current leadership challenges or aspirations.
This chapter was engaging and supported my learning.
The facilitator was effective in delivering the content and supporting your learning.
I feel confident in my ability to apply what I learned to real workplace challenges.
This experience is consistent with the values and culture we strive to embody in our everyday work.
Which topics or tools have had the biggest impact on your thinking or leadership so far?
What would improve your experience in the remaining sessions?

answers.text: everything in the content had the biggest impact, I wasn't aware of the difference is both ways of thinking
The success of my team in the company.
To train the exchange between player and learner in communication.
Reminder of TELUS culture and the role we all play in a productive and rewarding company..
The different behaviors that were discussed so far, its given me a different perspective on Leadership.
I still think that practical exercises are the best teachers, until you try what you have learned yourself, at least from my perspective you have not learned it.

Having a player minded way of thinking expanded my vision. I often tend to vent, blame others which indicates victim minded and knower. Taking responsibility and thinking creatively is a great solution for me to think about.
Having more day to day examples
So far all the topics and tools that have been discussed is basic leadership mentality.
A deeper dive into real actions and training beyond entry level leadership training.
Dimensions of success, but also likes the generative tools you are using in some chapters that made me feel like I was talking to a real human but keeping all situational question confidential
plenary discussions were insightful, more of that

Learning about the Knower/Learner mindset made me realize that sometimes I look at situations with a knower mindset. I need to strive more to be a learner.  Successfully applying this concept involves consciously choosing curiosity over certainty, viewing challenges as growth opportunities, and remaining open to feedback and diverse perspectives.

No comments at this point, but wanted to share that the breakout discussions during our online learning session were particularly enlightening. It was reassuring to discover that my peers shared similar experiences and challenges. These small group conversations created a valuable space for authentic connection, helping me realize that my professional journey resonates with others on the team. This shared understanding not only validated my experiences but also fostered a stronger sense of community within our virtual learning environment.

I've found that the victim-player mindset dynamic has prompted a fair amount of self reflection. There are certainly instances where I have attributed shortcomings to factors I can't explain or can't control. I can take more ownership of those in the future.
The live practice sessions. Being able to apply it in live scenarios - role playing.
all the mindsets and the videos where they are exemplified and also the reflections
to add a couple more examples of how to apply the right approach with a team/person
the different mindset and how much of an impact they can have., without even using them consciously
Player Victim Mindset, 3 dimensions of I, WE and IT
more time to balance my day to day task and the learning activity
The Knower-Learner module has been enlightening
Maybe a review of the pre-requisite modules would help refresh everyone's memory before going out on breakout sessions
I understand better the Player and victim mindsets and how they are related with the knower and learner ones. I'll focus more on the way to manage these mindsets on my team and myself
The importance to manage all situations on a reasonable way
I liked the I-WE-IT way of measuring success.
I enjoy the videos, but I would like some more interactive segments. I find gamification and interaction helps topics stick in more.
Victim Player and Knower vs Learner
To be mindful when I am allowing my own personal emotions and values impact my thinking, reasoning and my response to my team, situations and expectations
Have more relatable scenarios to listen, reflect and apply application
To have more practice and hear my peers' opinions.
I appreciate the reflection exercises. Really having to think through previous/current scenarios and apply the learning is very helpful in making the topics relevant,
I appreciate more of the media to read and digest vs the role playing videos.
To experience that weare all sitting in the same boat and facing the same struggles in our leadership
The break out room experience was very worthy, but too short. It was ok with approx. 4 to 5 persons in one room, but at the end of the day over 150 participants is way to much in my opinion.
That it is ok to be wrong and if we are open on those topics, but really honest our team will also see things differently.
Having more time to discuss with out peers.
Understanding the mindset of another person, background and strategies to overcome and objections while offering to support.
Have visibility on other actions to take when negative responses.
the I WE IT method for sure, it helped me see that everything could have different perspectives and still be true to itself.
I wish the reading courses had a read-aloud feature.
I-WE-IT really challenged me to reflect on my approach and mindset when approaching work.
I'd like for prompts to remain visible in break-out rooms - of course, I can also grab a screen-shot before the break-out.
The Player and learner mindset, sometimes we tend to complain about things without even knowing the facts behind them
More videos and more consise lectures as sometimes there can be too much text
Overall, English descriptions often sound very ambiguous and it is hard for me to understand. I would appreciate if descriptions are more direct and easy to be understood.
The Learner-Knower model. Now I start to realize that sometimes I tend to be more on the Knower side of the table and not respecting enough the other side opinion on the matter.
More practical cases in which Sebastian actually uses Emily's guidance.
The CB assessment helped me realize areas in my work as a leader, that I could improve. The first session with the facilitator supported my learning regarding unconscious bias and beliefs.
I would be happy to see more examples from the online lessons during the meetings with facilitator.
Taking other people's views and opinions into consideration before making decisions
To learn about the different mind set and learn how to identify to be able to acknowledge and adapt to change it to better the impact on me and my team
In the all up meeting after module, have more time in breaking room to truly explore, interact and learn from others in the training and not be pressurized by time
Watching the video's of real world examples and seeing what is suggested as positive versus negative.
More examples of how to solve or work within these challenges.
Learning more about the mindsets and realizing that unknowingly I could be affecting my team by not using the correct mindset.

While I understand breakout rooms help us discuss experiences, I'd like for the facilitator to open the floor to all attendees, ask for an specific anecdote or problem, and help us navigate it in real time.
It has been very eye-opening to learn about specific mindsets that may be unconscious and how becoming aware of them could great improve my performance as a project leader.
I would like more guided facilitation in the break out sessions to ensure we are getting the most from the concepts.
The knower mindset and the victim mindset and the I-WE-IT.
Maybe for the trainer to go into each of the breakout rooms to ensure the group are on track
More time in the breakout rooms with assistance from the experts
Talk openly about learner and knower situations
Victim and Player examples has really helped me visualize situations I have been and where do I stand
How can we react when dealing with someone lets says who is a knower, who doesn't want to listen to you. Is great to recognize this but how can we approach people like this?
I would like to  hear more from the facilitator
Disagreement being delivered in amiable ways with smart goals.
importance of setting and aligning mindsets
The victim-player mindset and the knower-learner mentality
Nothing comes to mind on how to improve my experience, given the context in which we all are going through these sessions (daily responsibilities)
the victim vrs player mindset topic
Victim player and Knower - Learner, it helped me identify some AOOs from my side, and understand my team's perspective.
Make it more interactive, homework, practice, etc.
More tips or best practices to develop the learner approach
Self awareness, be open to new don't be attached just to prev experiences but to try new things

All the reflections presented in the videos and the exercises we performed have helped me identify areas of opportunity for my leadership, and I've already seen the results of the training in my daily life.
I'm feeling really comfortable with the training so far, maybe more practice with the rest of the attendees.

the pace is good so far. i like that reminders are dropped in our chatrooms, too. breakout rooms are helping us realize more that others are experiencing some of the challenges we are facing or we faced. Providing and getting insights from our peers in these rooms made the session more meaningful
the I-WE-IT and the Victim Player topics
I believe that having sessions that help you put in practice what has been learned are also valuable to check for understanding. I would like more case scenarios to exercise what has been learned.

The OCI tool to represent both the Current Culture and the Desired Culture, So in retrospective I can identify the gap between these two and aim to detect which styles should be strengthened and which is advisable to discourage or minimize to achieve a good transformation.
a little bit more time in the small workshops with the rest of the participants during the Live sessions. :)
Having us share our opinion regarding topics
Both the player mindset and the learner mindset topics
A way to use learnings from different chapters in few situations where any of the mindsets could come into play in the same situation
Reviewing examples of situations in which I can clearly see from the outside the impact of certain actions.
the efficient communications and the team work improving
Reflection: by using the learner mindset you give the option to the team member to feel understood and listened but at the same time we can create a good collaboration and achieve the desired goal
Examples of how to do things whenever we will face any type of challenges
I really appreciated the Action Plan advice to help with my effectiveness in leadership.
Reflection time to review my notes and action plan as well as practice by putting the lessons learned into action.
the different mindsets that we can have right or wrong on different situations
not letting my anger kill my curiosity.
Be more open to hear peoples feedback and not being a Knower
I think will be good if as the company leaders we can have some values sessions in a quartely basis, that can help us to stay in the right path
Different personality types discussed. Player - Victim and Knower - Learner
Discussions with a fixed group of people to help track progress
The KI-Tools have been very interesting. The other concepts have not been new to me and are already part of my day to day business and how I do approach things.
All of the tools that help identify my personal traits and how they actually apply in the real workplace.
More information specific to improving on subconscious behaviours
None. I have a good experience so far.
The ICB profile test and tha Ai coach. Along with the discussion on the Knower vs the Learner mindset. Made me realise that in some case I act as a Knower
I would keep it as is, the format is great
The videos are good and help a lot
more questions about the things learned
I like the Victim-Player and Knower-Learner mindset combos!
Can't think of anything right as the sessions have exceeded my learning expectations.
Leaner Mindeset along with ability to see if i as leader is playing victim where i can be player along with knower
the videos help me understand the topics better
I'm afraid i missed the last session
Seeing the world as a leader
I can say - everything I saw
Learning through application, and the circle of behaviors.
the way you approach the people will determine the results you will get. Humility is a resource that will pick you up.
Know-er and Learner mindset and what they are and how to identify it

I think the recurring theme of objectively studying how you respond to business/team challenges has had the largest impact. It's shown me the value of setting time aside to reflect more often even though we're so busy and its easy to just move from one situation to the next
I would like to see how others are leveraging their AI to become more conscious leaders.
not so much time between the sessions
What I like the most are the videos explaining how to use the learner method
Left hand column, Core truth and The ladder of inference
More practical examples, in the e-learning and in the live sessions.
I believe I'll be more in to learning and sharing my thoughts as knower however with a positive mindsets to accept the feedback of nay modifications required in my thoughts or plan.
Treat each other with respect, listen, and communicate on an equal level.
Prepare for critical discussions and develop strategies to conduct them.
The knower and the learner, however knowing about my left hand column made me really curious
The sessions are going well so far; no changes.
the ladder of inference but in general all content
left hand column and ladder if inference.
smaller groups as 150 plus people in one session is not the best approach
all the different insights and the core truth
that we had more clarification of topics during the practice sessions
I think Modules 5 and 6 spoke to me the most and I think it'll benefit not just me but my team as well.
I believe that hearing about others' experiences will give me better insight into the situations faced by different departments.
Having the space to share experiences with people around the worlds, is unique, it gives you different perspectives.
Nothing that I can think of
Knower vs. learner and victim vs player mindset, ladder of inference, left hand column
not really to improve but wanted to share that i enjoyed the small group session. it was very engaging.
The exercises where you don't submit and just contemplated should be discussed lived to drive more of an active learning experience
Understanding the different leadership styles - it helped me understand I am mainly applying the Learner principles, which made feel more confident in my style.
Nothing that comes to mind at the moment.
this is about the practice and use on regular base at work and life
Really well defined strategies for more effective communication in difficult conversations!
Everything seems on track for a good experience
How to interact with other and with myself
Real assets to do knowledge checks .
I just wanted to know the similarity & difference, between Authentic Communication and so-called Assertive Communication

Every single one - however the tools in the communication module were particularly effective. Having the language for the concepts helps to be more conscious of unhelpful behaviours or mindsets and make improvements. Detoxifying the left-hand column, being aware of the ladder of inference and how to advocate and inquire productively is extremely eye opening.

The live coaching session with Michael Cunanan was brilliant and helps to understand concepts and get insights into how leaders handle situations. I would love more content like this and more facilitator lead content as opposed to group sessions.
more interactive sessions - I look forward to the group sessions
How to be an advocate and inquirer to analyze different point of views and have a unified solution that can benefit all parties
This open my mind to understand and be in both scenarios when you are the mediator and participant of a situation to be resolved with your team
The ladder of inference - this helps me understand and connect more with others and their opinions
I've found the "Left Hand Column" module to be practical
The course prompts a lot of self-reflection - it has been eye-opening!
Self-awareness and introspective on how to manage a better approach.
understanding and being aware of my "left hand"

Inference ladder
Core truth 
Left hand column
Going deeper into the certain topics from the course during the sessions - facilitator explanation
The different mindsets we have and always the topic for the assumptions we have
Purely graphically - sometimes the text goes all the way to the right side of the screen and it's being hidden by the scroller. It is impossible to read it and focus on it.

I think I have become more confident, especially in reflecting on the I-WE-IT aspects and the learner mindset and being aware of victim/ player mindset differences. I have been more aware of my left-hand column and finding my truth core. I feel like I can allow my team to be more independent, open to learning and learn from doing, rather than me micro-managing to ensure they do everything the way I would.

I find that sometimes I di not have all the experiences that are being discusses so it can be difficult to share, so to be mindful that not everyone is at the same level or in the same type of roles. I really enjoyed the smaller session.
More videos to support the theory
Given my role in the company, the advocate and enquire is one strong tool that can carry out significant positive impact in my day to day

My feedback so far in my Beyond experience remains the same, the topics and strategies covered are exclusively basic leadership skills and do not go beyond basic. For a course that it meant to be for leaders with seniority and experience to advance their skills and knowledge it is extremely slow and simple.
left hand concepts were key for me.
a bit more of interaction with the chatbot.
more plenary discussions instead of small group, sometimes it's awkward
Some slides had too much text and can be repetitive
ladder of inference and core truth
how to always start with the facts before making asumptions
The Victim-Player, the Left Hand Column and Core Truth and the Ladder of Inference

The live sessions aren't really clarifying the information from these materials - at most, they take time from a busy schedule as we are put in session groups with other trainees - and while it's nice to share our experiences, I don't feel it's actually productive in terms of learning the concepts better.
the Left Hand column and the core truth
Words and advise from a mentor
Suggest more material to read after receiving a lesson, books, authors or videos.
Listening to understand and the ladder of inference
To listen intently and to respect others opinions
how to deal with difficult conversations and achieving the team objectives
Left Hand Column and Core Truth
To maybe remove some of the non-participative people which makes the experience less valuable.
I have flagged that there are some misspellings in the class
left hand column, facts vs opinion vs inferences
more time and structure in the break out sessions
Dealing with situation in positive manner
left hand column and effective commitment and request.
Everything we have done so far has opened my mind to understanding a lot more
More information from the presenter instead of sharing so much thoughts!
ladder of inference was huge, this is so common and I didn't know it had a name so knowing about it will enable my ability to master it!
Keep up with work and these courses
I have learned more about positioning
The apology and complaint classification, and what makes for a good example of both, is helpful. I am going to work more of this into interactions with my team members.
The ladder of inference, it's incredible how we react to assumptions and the power of them over our decisions.
Core Truth and ladder of inference
Tha ladder of influence and victim player
KNower LEarner, Left Hadn Column, Ladder of Inference
Sometimes you feel the content very light, not too deep maybe you can suggest additional materials to deep any knowledge
Effective apology or effective complaint, focus my energy on the solution of a problem not on the impossible side but the options.
the decision and commitment process and the way to avoid not honor commitments
Have a clear response and commitment on requirements
how to formulate the responses for raising a complaint
some of the text in the material has grammatical errors.
My ability to be more focused and being positive while having any difficult conversation.
Steps for an effective apology or effective complaint
Building effective commitments and steps for effective apology will improve

I value the information related to Learner, Knower, Victim, Player. It's helped me reflect about my own behavior and management style while also presenting considerations about my own team, the team I work with, and the sales primes we support. It helps clarify reactions and guide discussions differently.
Not sure to be honest but I do like the small group sessions a lot.
The Left Hand Column as a way to reflect and discern toxic thoughts and solutions is really impactful. While I have not tried it specifically, I am grateful to have the tools to use moving forward.

The many clicks to get from one concept to the next is challenging for me. I suppose it is learner specific but I prefer the more direct approach of the earlier modules with less 'gamification' as part of the learning process. I find it distracting

The breakout room sessions usually people don't engage so the time is not well invested. I would say a different type of practice will be better like share the challenge of the weak or something like that.
Agreeing on how to make decisions
more time, this session had a lot of material but due to summer holiday period it was very hard to catch up and stay on track with schedule

Everything is going well!
Thank you!
I-WE-IT and Victim and Player have impacted the  most
Section about Victim/Player and Advocate and Inquire
In the Live session, I would like to have more time in breaking room (smaller group) we often get cut on very interesting and impactfull discussion
Apply knowledge with current challenges to better understand application
9 Agreeing on How We Make Decisions
Discuss over more specific scenarios like we did in the Small Group Sessions
I We it - also this clear step by step for negotiation will be useful
the different insights, the ladder of inference and how to create a commitment
facilitators sharing best practices and experiences
I would love to memorize the flow charts but it's impossible, maybe just with practice... but can we get these in a presentation so they are accessible?
in the part of choosing the best apology for Caro, video in option B and video in option C was the same one. "Option C, being the correct response" didnt have an effective apology.
Understand the real need behind decision making and meaningful steps and preocess to come to a comitment
When is live session, allow more time for breaking room
Agreeing on how we make decisions
Breakout rooms have been very beneficial, meeting other people and learning from their own experiences
More time during breakout room exercises
Core truths and ladder of inference
more interactive AI chats these are helpful
How to deal with broken commitments
The effective and ineffective part of making decisions
The "Core Belief" piece and also just breaking down things into facts have been particularly helpful.
Better time management on my part to be able to always apply myself 100%. The course itself is great.
Inference model, the profile test, the learner mindset etc... All is very valuable for me. I really enjoy the AI coach, this is a neutral party whith which we can really test the concepts
The art of an effective complaint and apology. Good framework for managing these.
The content on the modules is a little long winded, I'd prefer them to be a little more interactive. Clicking on different things, hearing sound clips, animations etc. help improve attention.
all of them but the Ladder of Inference and Core Truth to me are the ones that will most help me
Commitments, realistic requests and proper follow up.
I've enjoyed the most recent topics, nothing to add.
is not what you say but how to say it
The details regarding how to address different situations, using deadlines, get clarification, etc. sometimes we receive and provide too general information
Whats important for other person and me.
I loved the left hand Column and the core truth
I enjoy fast paced and simple example to understand the basics and then delve into a more complex situation to find the value on the application
Victim-Player, Knower-Learner and Advocate and Inquire
Better examples in form of videos.
learner mindset-victim mindset-conflict resolution-position and intent discovery
Knower / Learner mindset and Victim / Player
Tailored 1 on 1 coaching session with professional coach.
Would be great to cover a topic in 1 video so we can be focuse instead of going in between the pieces
The AI for difficult conversation is great!
Understanding What's Important for the Other person and for me
How to request without engaging into bad conversations but instead requesting with a mean and positiveness.

Modules 9 -12 so far have been incredibly useful for me in my role as a project manager as the majority of our role involves the commitment cycle. I have certainly taken ALOT from these modules: agreeing on decision making, constructive vs destructive escalation, position vs interest, effective requests and acceptable responses and effective complaints and apologies! Feeling excited about making improvements to my thinking and leadership in these areas!
Having a learner mindset and acting as a player
Effective Requests, Commitments and what to do when we can't keep our promises
more visual presentation of new concepts
Making an effective request and an acceptable apology for a missed commitment
Agreeing Who Does What and When
The different phases of a meeting, I will use what I learn on my future meetings
The materials and videos are great, I expect to have more material techniques, examples and material that can help me to improve my leadership skills.
Left hand column, ways to connect to make other people feel heard and ways to handle difficult conversation.
It's all very helpful to revamp the mindset completely.

I like the videos that portray a real-life scenario by acting it out. It really makes me feel connected to the story and how these concepts can look like in real life. Thank you for putting so much effort in these!
Perhaps activities that involve more interactivity and not just multiple choice questions.
Left Hand Column and Core Truth
Understanding what's important for the other person and for me
none. actually learning a lot from the sessions.
How we make decisions, Building effective commitments, apology or complaint
I have learned a lot about how to conduct a meeting, in the check in and check out.
All good, I love these sessions.
Victim Player topic had an impact
Advocate and enquire impacted on my thinking
How to make decisions on limited available information
Ways to agree to commitment and tailored means to respond in case you fail.
What is and how to develop team effectiveness?
2 hours session with a personal coach to personalize and discuss specific areas for improvement.
building effective teams and the different insights
I prefer more instructor led content than breakout groups
How to conduct an Effective meeting.
Topics around making effective commitments and how to responds when a breach happens was helpful.
So many!! Mindsets, Core Thruth, Decision making process and effective meetings.
More time in break outt rooms! :)
Working with team and committing on deadlines
Mindset Awareness and reflection on the I-WE-IT
More case studies and tricky situations for review so I can apply my learnings
Check IN & Check out and Process of conducting effective meeting
Sharing content on video with examples
Case study from varied scenarios with different intensities
taking and owning accountability. Have a solutions driven mindset
how to approach being more efficient and effective in our day to day activities and the impact of meetings.
better scenarios and videos to be more engaging.
More relevant situations as an example
I loved the fact of Learning Challenge
Having an one o one approach
How to develop team effectiveness, Advocate & Inquire, Core truth
Evaluate real scenarios with the cahnge of command
The player approach, strategies for the buy-in after the meetings
I need to have clear objectives, communicate them, and follow through with a specific action plan where everyone knows what their part entails.
Defining the kind of meeting ahead of time for people to prepare mentally I think is a good practice.

I find it harder to engage with the AI driven videos compared to when we had actual people involved. I understand it's great for efficiency of creating material, but it's not as watchable or as easy to stay focused on the material.
have knowledge checks to make sure we apply what we have learned
all the topics are very relevant for a leaders, how we can make or meeting effective & productive it's very essential for a leader.
mindeset - what kind of mindset i - as individual brings and effective meetings
To adopt the new practices learned in my daily habits.
To put in practice the steps of the listening process
Meeting closure is as important as the opening!
Tools to use when I can't keep with my commitments
Everything has been very helpful to give me a different perspective on leadership and building relationships.
The way to manage changes and emotions developed during the process
Agreeing who does what and when
Question & emotions, because when I identify the emotion now I can think of the questions to ask.
the ladder of inference and the mindsets
check in mostly, I think sometimes we are in a rush and we forget to see how the rest of the people in the meeting are
adding up more details to the call and incorporate the check ins
value one can get out of implementing effective meetings with your team
Defining who does what and when is very important as part of my job
I loved the fact on how to decode our emotions
It helped me to organize my ideas and emotions
There are meetings that would only derail the team because we sometimes are unclear about the true purpose
How to manage meetings effectively, how to manage feedback effectively.
Self awereness and how to react to emotions.
There are no more chapters but i enjoyed the lessons.
Recognizing and Decoding emotions is a helpful topic. I like it!
Managing effective meetings is key for my role as PM
The methodology so far is working perfectly
Emotional intelligence and ways to decode different emotions. Further the how to bridge gaps between your emotions and others on any new change.
I think no change needed as such just if we can keep on getting regular videos or lessons even after completing this course will help us recall the right behaviors as a people leaders.
More experience sharing from people who have gone through specific challenges and how they have gone through
I-WE-IT,  Left hand column, core truth, Advocate and inquire, change and emotions, who and when does what, effective meetings.
decoding emotions and strategies to manage emotions
Developing team effectiveness and conducting effective meetings.
Why is important to keep promises and how this impacts the team performance and their perception
Change & emotions, Knower-Learner, Left hand column
Adding few more examples and videos
Player instead of Victim, Emotions are important in Business.
Learner mindset and the module about decoding emotions. It's great to be part of the change were leadership and validation becomes a real super power instead of what was in the past.
More examples of decoding emotions, or even a module of how important is psychology and leadership together, besides the old power, leading through validation and a more human leader
The entire content is great, as it provides practical principles and tools. I also appreciated the mention of the author and the source of this knowledge, which is helpful for reading related books.

As we are landing on this course to complete it, I would like to know about strategies/tips to continue polishing all this knowledge in the day-to-day. Having the certificate is not a guarantee that you are applying 100% the whole course

The concept of understanding emotions is something I became interested in years ago, and realizing that emotions are simply tools our bodies and minds use to solve (not aggravate) our day-to-day challenges has been a game changer.
More references or more scientific information about emotions, for some analytical thinkers it makes more sense to think about science and easier to understand
Understanding the other person and making decision
how to deal with the emotions of people and my own

Loved the genAI chat we used at the beginning, it helpped me a lot to understand me better and discover AOO and how to better work on them. Also in the the last module of emotions, loved that we have a file questions to ask depending on the emotion expressed.
The topics regarding the left hand column
The video presentations on Digital Transformation (Incentive - Habit) was great.  I enjoyed the "story behind emotions" as well.

For the axialent course material, the AI ones really didn't hit the spot. The in person sessions and real human presentations were great. I would like to hear more about actual implementations and success stories too. The break-out rooms interestingly, although useful for some reasons, end up taking up time where I feel I don't hear enough about successes and real world successful implementation of the tools and learnings.

There is more then meet the eye with you are in a position of leadership, supporting human being. Staying attentive and open to identify mindset, behaviors that can help you lead and support with a clear understanding of challenges and key action to take.
Decoding emotions, I just took my emotions at face value in the past
Creating a plan for an effective and productive meeting.

The quality of the AI-generated videos is a significant concern, as the frequent buffering issues are negatively impacting my learning experience. The constant interruptions and poor playback performance are draining my energy and enthusiasm for the content. This technical shortcoming is hindering my ability to engage with the material effectively and maintain focus throughout the lessons. It's unclear why AI-generated content would suffer from such playback issues, but addressing these technical problems is crucial to ensure a smooth and engaging learning process.
Conducting Effective Meetings and The Change Curve
exercises with people having the same lessons
Agreeing on how to make decisions
Victim Player Mindset, 3 Dimensions, Left Hand Column, Core Truth
While I acknowledge the challenge, I am committed to developing greater emotional awareness and empathy in my interactions with others.
I really liked the slide on the tools for digital transformation, and I really liked the reflections the mindsets trigger
make sure to align content to digital transformation
The explanation what is behind the emotions and what is happening if you don't act
Recognizing the emotion first in order to act properly
I would like to include more knowledge checking points that actually provides a score per module
the I - We - It/Be - Do - Have
Effective meetings meanly how to make them effective
More quizez and different speaker since the last one's pace did not keep me engaged
Ladder of Inference, Advocacy & Enquiry, Commitment & Decision Making & different Mindsets
Looking beyond the immediate surface of difficulties and understanding reasons and strategies to cope.

If there will be self paced modules it's much better for them to include real people talking in real situations (like an extraction of a training session). It's much more engaging rather then just reed some bullet pointed text.
the WE, the IT and I, also the mindset change from knower, to learner and player
